title: Extract shared App integration test helpers
description:
Parent context: sheetspace-865.

Problem / goal:
frontend/src/App.test.tsx contains reusable setup and interaction helpers mixed with test cases. Before splitting the file, extract those helpers so each future thematic test file can stay concise and consistent.

Implementation scope:
- Move workbook factories out of App.test.tsx into frontend/src/test/workbookFactories.ts or an equivalently named test helper module.
- Move API test doubles out of App.test.tsx into frontend/src/test/apiClients.ts or an equivalently named helper module. Include autosaveClient, persistedWorkbookClient, and deferred promise support.
- Move App UI actions and queries into frontend/src/test/appScreen.tsx or equivalent. Include helpers such as workspaceSurface, createSheetFromToolbar, openCellEditor, openSheetContextMenu, and resizeHandle.
- Move DOM geometry helpers into frontend/src/test/domGeometry.ts or equivalent. Include testRect and reusable workspace rect/scroll stubs where useful.
- Update App.test.tsx to import these helpers without changing test behavior.

Important constraints:
- This bead should be test-only. Do not change production behavior.
- Keep helpers small and boring. Prefer named functions over a large class hierarchy.
- Helpers should perform setup or named user actions; keep behavior assertions in the test files.
- Avoid making helpers so generic that test intent becomes hidden.

Acceptance criteria:
- App.test.tsx no longer defines the extracted helper functions inline.
- Existing App tests still pass with helper imports.
- Helper module names and exported functions are clear enough for future agents to use without reading all of App.test.tsx.
- No App test cases are moved to new files in this bead unless unavoidable for import correctness.

Suggested check:
- cd frontend && npm test -- App.test.tsx

Implementation scope:
- Create App.formulas.test.tsx or equivalent.
- Move App-level tests for raw formula preservation while editing, mixed-case/spaced/multiline formula display, cross-sheet formula display, formula error cells being selectable/editable, formula recomputation after referenced cell edits, uncommitted edit text not recomputing until commit, editing formula cells back to another formula or plain content, and formulas becoming valid after appending rows/columns.
- Keep parser/evaluator correctness in workbook.test.ts. App.formulas.test.tsx should verify rendered behavior through <App /> and user actions only.
- Keep the existing sheet rename preserving raw formula text either here or in App.sheetManagement.test.tsx, but do not duplicate it in both places.
- Leave a clear place for sheetspace-wlh multiline formula editor tests if that bead has not already added them.

Important constraints:
- Do not change formula parsing/evaluation behavior.
- Preserve the distinction between displayed formula results outside edit mode and raw stored formula text inside edit mode.
- Keep formula error cells editable and avoid weakening that coverage.

Acceptance criteria:
- Formula App integration tests live in a dedicated file.
- Formula parser/evaluator details remain in workbook.test.ts and are not duplicated unnecessarily in App-level tests.
- App.test.tsx is further reduced and no longer owns formula behavior.
- The frontend test suite passes.

Suggested check:
- cd frontend && npm test

title: Render sheet frames with identity and independent positions
description:
Context:
Each sheet in Sheetspace is a movable object on the workspace. The MVP needs identifiable frames before dragging, grid editing, rename, and persistence can be useful. Broader frame features such as resizing, z-order management, collapse, and grouping are future scope.

What to do:
Render each sheet inside a visible frame at its own workspace x/y position. Include a header bar with enough identity to distinguish sheets and a body area reserved for the grid. Keep the frame surface minimal and compatible with later dragging, grid rendering, and sheet rename.

Acceptance criteria:
- Every sheet in workbook state renders as a distinct frame.
- Frames use each sheet position rather than a fixed stack or tab list.
- Frame headers display the current visible sheet name.
- Multiple frames can be visible at different positions.
- Frame rendering does not require cells to contain values.
- MVP does not add frame resizing, z-order controls, collapse/minimize/hide, or grouping.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests cover rendering zero, one, and multiple positioned frames, visible sheet names, and absence of grid/content requirements for frame rendering.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/sheet-frames.md, plan/features/workspace.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by workspace sheet creation.
close_reason: Completed frame rendering implementation and passed separate review

title: Make Delete and Backspace clear selected cell contents outside edit mode
description:
Problem / goal:
When a cell is selected but not being edited, pressing Delete or Backspace currently starts an empty edit. Users expect these keys to clear the selected cell contents immediately, while preserving normal text-editor behavior when already editing inside the cell editor.

Implementation scope:
- Update frontend grid keyboard handling so Delete and Backspace clear the active cell when focus is on a selected non-editing grid cell.
- Do not enter edit mode for Delete/Backspace outside edit mode.
- Preserve current textarea behavior while editing: Delete and Backspace should modify editor text/caret selection normally and should not trigger grid-level clearing.
- Preserve existing autosave/commit behavior expectations when a stored cell is cleared.

Out of scope:
- Multi-cell selection clearing unless already supported by current selection model.
- Redesigning edit start behavior for printable keys, Enter, or F2.

Acceptance criteria:
- With a non-editing selected cell containing content, Delete clears the content immediately.
- With a non-editing selected cell containing content, Backspace clears the content immediately.
- Empty selected cells remain empty and do not enter edit mode on Delete/Backspace.
- While editing a cell, Delete and Backspace behave like normal textarea editing keys.
- Focus and active selection remain usable after clearing.
- Add or update focused frontend tests for both non-editing clear behavior and editing text behavior where practical.

Plan references:
- docs/plan/PROJECT_VISION.md: familiar spreadsheet editing comes first.

Testing expectations:
- Run relevant frontend tests and compile/test target for touched code.

title: Implement workspace pan and zoom interactions
description:
Context:
The MVP must prove the spatial loop by letting users pan around a 2D workspace and zoom in/out. The broader workspace brief mentions viewport persistence, navigation history, reference lines, search, and minimaps, but those are not part of the MVP acceptance criteria.

What to do:
Add workspace viewport state and interactions for panning and zooming. Keep controls discoverable and testable, and ensure sheet frames remain positioned in workspace coordinates as the viewport changes. Keep the implementation scoped to pan/zoom only.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can pan the workspace.
- User can zoom the workspace in and out.
- Frame positions remain stable in workspace coordinates while viewport transforms change.
- Pan/zoom interactions do not interfere with cell editing or frame dragging once those features exist.
- Zoom limits prevent unusable extreme scales.
- MVP does not add viewport persistence, navigation history, minimap, search, command palette navigation, or formula reference lines.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests cover viewport state changes through available controls/events, frame rendering after pan/zoom, coordinate behavior needed by frame dragging, and zoom limit behavior. Manual test notes may cover pointer-wheel behavior if jsdom cannot fully exercise it.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/workspace.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by workspace and frame rendering.

comments.author: zoltan
comments.text: Review completed by separate subagent: no blocking or non-blocking issues found. Checks passed this turn: make -C frontend compile; make -C frontend test (78 tests); git diff --check. Residual risk: future frame dragging should use the viewport coordinate conversion added here.

title: Handle formula saves that reference pending sheets
description:
Problem / goal:
After formulas store sheet-qualified references by backend UUID, formula edits that reference optimistic pending sheets need safe save behavior. A formula must not be persisted with a live `pending:<uuid>` target while that sheet may still receive a backend UUID, but the user edit should still be saved as a broken reference if the pending target is deleted before it is ever saved.

Implementation scope:
- Detect committed formula edits that reference an unsaved `pending:<uuid>` sheet.
- Defer saving those formula edits while the referenced pending sheet may still receive a backend UUID.
- When the referenced pending sheet is saved, rewrite the deferred reference to the backend-supplied UUID and save the formula edit.
- If the referenced pending sheet is deleted before it is saved, save the formula edit as a broken reference that evaluates to `#REF!`.
- The implementation may persist the unresolved `pending:<uuid>` token, or another explicit broken-reference representation, only for the deleted-before-save case where no backend UUID can ever exist.
- Ensure a persisted broken reference for a deleted-before-save pending sheet never resolves to a future unrelated sheet.
- Integrate with the pending-id remap and queued-work behavior from sheetspace-cqe.

Out of scope:
- Core formula reference storage by backend UUID; that belongs to sheetspace-nyz.
- Creating durable offline queue semantics or backend idempotency.
- Treating `pending:<uuid>` as a resolvable live sheet reference after persistence.
- Formula grammar expansion, reference navigation UI, or formula refactoring commands.

Acceptance criteria:
- Saving a formula that references an unsaved pending sheet is deferred while that sheet may still become saved.
- When the referenced pending sheet is saved, the deferred formula edit is saved using the backend-supplied sheet UUID.
- If the referenced pending sheet is deleted before it is saved, the formula edit is still saved and evaluates as `#REF!`.
- Persisted formula data never treats a `pending:<uuid>` token as a live sheet id.
- A broken reference created from a deleted-before-save pending sheet cannot later resolve to an unrelated sheet.
- Unrelated pending-sheet mutations and unrelated formula saves continue to use existing queue semantics.

Plan references:
- docs/plan/PROJECT_VISION.md
- docs/plan/mvp.task.md
- docs/plan/features/formulas.md
- docs/plan/features/persistence.md

Testing expectations:
- Tests cover pending referenced sheet save deferral, remapping deferred formula saves to backend UUIDs, pending referenced sheet deletion saving the formula as `#REF!`, preventing later accidental resolution of the broken pending reference, and preserving unrelated queued work.
- Include frontend queue/controller tests and any parser/persistence tests needed by the chosen representation.
- Run make test and make compile, or document narrower checks if the full suite is not practical.

title: Implement single-cell selection and sheet focus transitions
description:
Context:
The MVP grid only needs single-cell selection, but selection must work across multiple sheets and support committing active edits when focus moves to another cell or sheet. Error-cell selection is verified later when formula display exists.

What to do:
Add UI state and interactions for selecting one cell at a time. Track the active sheet/cell and update selection when the user clicks another cell or another sheet. Keep range, row, and column selection out of scope.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can select a single cell in a sheet.
- Selection visibly identifies the active cell.
- Selecting another cell moves selection to that cell.
- Selecting a cell in another sheet moves active sheet/cell focus.
- Empty, text, numeric-looking, and formula cells are selectable through the same path.
- Range, row, and column selection are not added in MVP.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests cover selecting cells, moving selection within a sheet, moving selection between sheets, and selecting cells with seeded text/formula content. Formula-error cell selection is covered by the formula display bead.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/grid-editing.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by grid rendering.
close_reason: Completed selection implementation, checks passed, reviewed

title: Refactor App UI integration tests by feature area
description:
Problem / goal:
frontend/src/App.test.tsx has grown into a 2,453-line mixed-domain UI integration suite. The suite is valuable, but its current shape makes changes hard to review and easy to conflict. Split it into feature-oriented files, extract shared helpers, and move low-level behavior coverage to focused hook/model tests where appropriate.

Desired end state:
- App-level tests remain user-facing integration tests around <App /> workflows.
- Shared setup and action helpers live under frontend/src/test/ rather than being embedded in every App test file.
- App integration tests are split by behavior area: startup, autosave, workspace frames, grid navigation, cell editing, formulas, sheet management, and one broad MVP workflow smoke test.
- Detailed queue, geometry, workbook-controller, parser/evaluator, and keyboard intent behavior is covered in lower-level tests instead of duplicated through <App /> where possible.
- The refactor preserves behavior and should not intentionally change product code.

Relevant files:

- frontend/src/SheetGridCell.test.tsx
- frontend/src/workbook.test.ts

Acceptance criteria:

- frontend tests pass after the split.
- No feature behavior is changed as part of this refactor.
- Each resulting App integration test file has a single clear reason to change and is small enough for ordinary review.

Out of scope:
- Implementing new product behavior.
- Changing the app architecture beyond test-only helper extraction.
- Adding Playwright unless a later bead explicitly asks for browser-level visual testing.

title: Recompute formula display results after workbook edits
description:
Context:
The MVP requires formula evaluation to update after committed workbook edits while preserving raw formula text for editing. Frontend recalculation is authoritative and optimistic; it does not wait for backend autosave success. Rename-triggered recomputation depends on the rename behavior existing first.

What to do:
Wire formula evaluation into committed workbook state updates so formula display values refresh after relevant changes. A full-workbook recompute is acceptable for MVP; do not introduce dependency-graph optimization unless it is already the simplest local implementation.

Acceptance criteria:
- While a cell is in edit mode, the edit text is shown and not used for formula evaluation until commit.
- Committing an input cell edit updates formula display results that depend on it.
- Committing a formula cell edit updates its own parsed/evaluated display result.
- Editing a formula back to non-formula content removes formula display behavior for that cell.
- Sheet rename triggers recomputation against current visible sheet names; formulas using old names show `#REF!`.
- Row/column append updates formula reference validity against the latest local dimensions.
- Formula errors are derived/rendered without replacing raw formula text.
- Recompute failures remain cell-level and do not prevent unrelated cells from displaying.
- No post-MVP reference visualizations or dependency graph UI are introduced.

Test expectations:
Unit or integration tests cover recompute after referenced cell edits, formula-to-value edits, value-to-formula edits, formula error updates, cross-sheet reference updates, rename-induced `#REF!`, row/column append effects on references, edit-mode non-recompute behavior, and unrelated formulas continuing after an error.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src, frontend/src/App.tsx, plan/features/formulas.md, plan/features/grid-editing.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by formula evaluator, cell editing state work, and sheet rename behavior.

title: Investigate App.mvpWorkflow timeout in full coverage test run
description:
Problem / goal:
The implementation of sheetspace-wlh repeatedly saw `make test` fail during the frontend coverage run because `App.mvpWorkflow.test.tsx` timed out at the default 10s limit. The same test passed when run standalone with `npm test -- --run App.mvpWorkflow.test.tsx`, so this appears to be a full-suite or coverage-run timing issue rather than a deterministic workflow failure.

What to investigate:
- Reproduce the timeout under `make test` / frontend coverage.
- Determine whether the broad MVP workflow test is too slow under coverage, whether recent editor changes added measurable overhead, or whether there is an async wait/user-event pattern that only stalls under the full suite.
- Decide whether to optimize the test, split the smoke workflow, raise the timeout intentionally, or adjust the tested interaction path.

Acceptance criteria:
- The cause of the timeout is understood and documented in the bead or implementation notes.
- `make test` completes reliably without relying on repeated retries.
- Any timeout increase is justified by test scope and not used to hide an async hang.

Context:
- Observed twice while working sheetspace-wlh on 2026-05-23.
- Backend tests passed before the frontend timeout.
- `npm test -- --run App.mvpWorkflow.test.tsx` passed standalone in about 3.3s.

title: Reconcile MVP plan wording with formula and persistence decisions
description:
Context:
The MVP plan still contains wording that predates the closed formula and persistence decision beads. In particular, `plan/mvp.task.md` says cross-sheet formula references may be supported when they fit naturally, while the closed formula decision and current MVP formula beads require cross-sheet references. The MVP plan should not contradict the active bead backlog.

What to do:
Update the MVP planning text so it reflects the recorded decisions without expanding implementation scope. Align the MVP plan with required cross-sheet SUM references, named sheet creation/rename behavior, raw formula preservation, rename-without-formula-rewrite behavior, and persistence of sheet names/renames alongside sheets, dimensions, cells, formulas, and positions.

Acceptance criteria:
- MVP plan no longer describes cross-sheet formula references as optional.
- MVP plan mentions visible sheet names where needed for cross-sheet formulas.
- MVP plan records that MVP rename does not rewrite formulas and old-name references display `#REF!` after recomputation.
- MVP persistence acceptance includes sheet ids, visible sheet names, renames, dimensions, raw cell content, raw formulas, and positions.
- The docs update does not add post-MVP features such as formula reference rewriting, reference visualization, viewport persistence, accounts, collaboration, import/export, or multiple workbooks to MVP scope.

Test expectations:
No code tests required. Review the edited MVP plan against the closed formula and persistence decision beads.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
plan/mvp.task.md, plan/features/formulas.md, plan/features/persistence.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by the recorded formula and persistence decisions.

title: Add MVP end-to-end regression coverage and final acceptance pass
description:
Context:
The MVP spans backend persistence, spatial workspace interactions, named sheet creation/rename, grid editing, cross-sheet formulas, structure changes, autosave status, and reload behavior. A final bead should verify the integrated user loop without expanding scope beyond the MVP plan.

What to do:
Add focused integration/regression coverage for the complete MVP workflow and run the project checks. Fix only issues needed to satisfy existing MVP beads and record any out-of-scope gaps as follow-up beads.

Acceptance criteria:
- Covered workflow creates multiple named sheets through the supported creation paths, arranges them, renames a sheet, edits values, enters same-sheet and cross-sheet SUM formulas, appends rows/columns, autosaves/reloads, and verifies restored raw state plus frontend-calculated display.
- Formula raw text remains verbatim through edit, autosave, and reload.
- Formula error cells remain selectable/editable during regression coverage.
- Rename does not rewrite formulas; formulas using old sheet names show `#REF!`.
- Startup load failure blocks editing with retry/error state.
- Autosave failure after successful load keeps editing available and shows app-level unsaved/error state.
- Backend and frontend tests pass.
- The root build/test workflow expected by the repo passes or any inability to run it is documented.
- No future-scope feature from the plan is required to pass MVP acceptance.

Test expectations:
Run make test and any relevant frontend/backend build commands. Add regression tests at the level best supported by the existing stack for named sheet creation, rename, cross-sheet formulas, autosave status, persistence reload, and formula-error editability.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
Makefile, frontend/src/App.test.tsx, frontend/src, backend/src/test/kotlin/com/sheetspace, plan/mvp.task.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by all user-facing MVP behavior and end-to-end persistence.

title: Evaluate SUM formulas with isolated cell-level errors
description:
Context:
Formula cells should show evaluated results outside edit mode. Errors in one formula must not crash the app, block UI interaction, or prevent unrelated formulas from computing. Frontend formula evaluation is authoritative for MVP; backend persistence stores raw workbook state only.

What to do:
Implement formula evaluation against a frontend workbook snapshot for the MVP parser output. SUM should aggregate numeric values from same-sheet and cross-sheet references/ranges according to the recorded strict numeric semantics. Return typed display results for successes and cell-level errors. Do not add non-SUM functions, arithmetic operators, broad Excel compatibility, backend calculation, or dependency-graph optimization.

Acceptance criteria:
- SUM evaluates same-sheet individual references and ranges.
- SUM evaluates cross-sheet individual references and ranges by current visible sheet name.
- SUM accepts a variable number of arguments.
- Empty referenced cells contribute 0.
- Plain decimal/integer values with optional leading minus sign contribute numeric values after trimming.
- Non-empty non-numeric referenced text causes `#VALUE!`.
- Unsupported functions, parse failures, missing sheets, out-of-bounds references, unresolved ranges, and value issues become the correct MVP cell-level error labels.
- Referenced formula errors propagate; if several errors are referenced, the first error in deterministic test-documented order is returned.
- Direct and indirect cycles, including cross-sheet cycles, display `#CYCLE!` for participating cells.
- A failure in one formula does not stop evaluation of unrelated formulas.
- Evaluation never replaces or normalizes raw cell formula text.

Test expectations:
Unit tests cover numeric SUM, mixed references and ranges, same-sheet and cross-sheet references, quoted sheet names, empty cells, strict text behavior, rejected numeric-like formats, referenced formula errors, unrelated formula independence, missing/invalid references, direct cycles, indirect cycles, and cross-sheet cycles.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src, plan/features/formulas.md, plan/features/grid-editing.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by formula parser.

title: Define MVP workbook state model and addressing helpers
description:
Context:
The MVP needs one coherent application model for workbooks, sheets, cell content, sheet positions, dimensions, sheet names, and evaluated cell display state before UI, formulas, and persistence can be wired together. New workspaces start empty. New sheets are created only from a user-provided valid name, start as empty 10-column by 20-row grids, and can be referenced by visible sheet name in formulas.

What to do:
Define the minimal MVP workbook/sheet/cell types and helper functions for sheet creation, sheet rename validation, cell addressing, Excel-style column labels, range expansion, and row/column append operations. Keep the model scoped to MVP state and compatible with backend persistence.

Acceptance criteria:
- A new workbook can represent an empty workspace with zero sheets.
- A new sheet requires a caller-provided visible name and is not auto-created or auto-named.
- Sheet ids are stable and sheet names are non-empty and unique within the workbook.
- A new sheet has a stable id, visible name, x/y position, 10 columns, 20 rows, and no stored cell values.
- Cell content stores raw user input verbatim, including formulas beginning with =, formula casing, whitespace, and line breaks.
- Helpers convert zero-based column indexes to labels including A, B, Z, AA, and AB.
- Helpers parse/normalize A1 cell coordinates and expand rectangular ranges inside sheet bounds.
- Helpers support sheet-name lookup for cross-sheet formula resolution by current visible sheet name.
- Helpers support validating sheet create/rename attempts without rewriting formulas.

Test expectations:
Unit tests cover empty workbook defaults, create-sheet validation, duplicate/empty sheet names, rename validation, new-sheet grid defaults, column labels past Z, A1 parsing, invalid addresses, range expansion, cross-sheet sheet-name lookup, and raw formula text preservation.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src, frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src/App.test.tsx, backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace, plan/mvp.task.md, plan/features/columns-rows-structure.md, plan/features/formulas.md

Dependencies/blockers:
None. This is a foundation bead for the MVP.
close_reason: Completed MVP workbook model and addressing helpers with frontend/backend tests

title: Move sheet frames by dragging header bars
description:
Context:
Moving a sheet in the MVP happens through its frame header bar. Sheet positions must update independently so users can arrange multiple worksheets spatially, and movement should respect the workspace pan/zoom coordinate system.

What to do:
Implement pointer dragging on frame headers to update a sheet frame x/y position in workbook state. Keep body interactions available for grid selection/editing rather than frame movement. Use workspace-coordinate deltas so the behavior remains correct after pan and zoom.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dragging a frame header moves that sheet.

title: Add frontend pending IDs for optimistic sheets
description:
Problem / goal:
After durable sheet IDs move to backend UUIDs, the frontend still needs responsive sheet creation. Add local pending sheet IDs that can be remapped safely when the backend create request resolves.

Implementation scope:
- Represent unsaved optimistic sheets with local-only ids shaped like `pending:<uuid>`.
- The suffix does not need to come from a specific UI UUID library, but every pending sheet id must be unique; never use the bare string `pending:` for multiple pending sheets.
- Ensure create-sheet requests send no sheet id.
- On create success, remap the pending id to the backend UUID across workbook state, active selection, layout state, autosave/edit queues, and any queued work that references the sheet.
- Queue mutations for a pending sheet behind the create request so saved mutations target the backend UUID.
- Handle deleting a pending sheet before create is sent, while create is in flight, and after create resolves.
- Drop or cancel queued work that only targets a pending sheet deleted before it becomes saved.

Out of scope:
- Backend idempotency for create retries.
- Durable offline queue semantics.
- Sheet deletion for already-saved sheets beyond the pending lifecycle hooks needed here.
- Formula reference storage by UUID.

Acceptance criteria:
- New sheets appear optimistically with unique local ids shaped like `pending:<uuid>` before the backend responds.
- No two pending sheets share the same id, and pending ids are never just the bare string `pending:`.
- POST /api/sheets requests still omit id.
- A successful create response atomically replaces the pending id with the backend UUID in all relevant frontend state and queued operations.
- Mutations made to a pending sheet persist after create resolves and are sent using the backend UUID.
- Deleting a pending sheet before create completion removes it locally and prevents obsolete queued work from being sent.
- Deleting a pending sheet after create was already sent leaves no resurrected sheet or stale queued mutations when the create resolves.

Plan references:
- docs/plan/PROJECT_VISION.md
- docs/plan/mvp.task.md
- docs/plan/features/sheet-frames.md
- docs/plan/features/persistence.md

Testing expectations:
- Frontend tests cover unique pending id creation, create request shape, remapping across state, queued pending mutations, pending deletion before create, pending deletion during in-flight create, and stale queue cleanup.
- Backend tests are only needed if API contracts changed during implementation.
- Run make test and make compile, or document narrower checks if the full suite is not practical.

- User-facing saved-sheet deletion controls and general optimistic saved-sheet deletion remain out of scope and belong to sheetspace-qtm.

title: Add frontend workbook API client and startup load lifecycle
description:
Context:

title: Wire frontend per-entity autosave queue and save status
description:
Context:
After the frontend can load the workbook and call backend per-entity APIs, committed MVP state changes need to autosave without blocking local editing. The MVP persistence decision defines app-level save status and per-entity queue semantics. MVP sheet frame state now includes position and explicit z-order.

What to do:
Wire committed workbook changes into per-entity autosave calls for sheet create, sheet rename, sheet position, sheet z-order, cell content, row append, and column append. Implement app-level save status and per-entity queueing: each entity key has at most one running save and one queued latest replacement; different keys may save in parallel.

Acceptance criteria:
- Committed sheet create, sheet rename, sheet position, sheet z-order, cell content, row append, and column append changes trigger the matching per-entity API call.
- Transient in-progress cell edits do not autosave until committed.
- Autosave failures after successful load keep the in-memory workbook editable and show an app-level save-failed/unsaved state.
- Save status is app-level only, with loading handled by the startup load bead and post-load states such as saving, saved, and save failed/unsaved.
- For each entity key, a new committed change replaces any queued unsent change for that key but does not cancel a request already running.
- Different entity keys may save in parallel.
- Stale save completions must not mark newer unsaved work as saved.
- Frontend formula evaluation remains optimistic and does not wait for backend save success.
- No explicit Save button, per-cell save indicator, account, collaboration, import/export UI, multi-workbook behavior, or durable offline queue is introduced.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests mock fetch for per-entity save calls, failed autosave after successful load, app-level save status, same-key queued replacement, parallel different-key saves, stale completion not marking newer work saved, no autosave for transient in-progress edits, and z-order autosave.

title: Make formula sheet references use sheet UUIDs only
description:
Problem / goal:
Formula references currently still depend too much on visible sheet names. Sheet names are user-facing labels and should not be formula identity. Cross-sheet formulas should resolve through stable sheet UUIDs so renaming a sheet does not change formula meaning, break formula evaluation, or require rewriting formulas by name.

Implementation scope:
- Make cross-sheet formula identity use sheet UUIDs only.
- Continue persisting sheet names as sheet display metadata, but never store or rely on sheet names as persisted formula/reference identity.
- When the user edits a formula using visible sheet names, resolve those display names to sheet UUIDs before saving.
- Persist cross-sheet formula reference metadata using sheet IDs as the canonical target identity.
- Render formula text for editing/display by mapping stored sheet IDs back to current visible sheet names.
- Ensure sheet rename updates only sheet display metadata and sheet revision; referenced formulas must keep resolving by UUID.
- Remove backend persistence fallback that derives formula reference identity from sheet names embedded in raw formula text.
- Keep missing/deleted target sheet behavior explicit: references to absent sheet IDs should evaluate/display as #REF!.

Out of scope:
- Do not add broad Excel formula compatibility.
- Do not implement formula reference rewriting on rename.
- Do not change row/column/cell identity semantics beyond what is required for sheet UUID references.
- Do not reshape mutation API responses in this bead; that is tracked separately.

Acceptance criteria:
- Persisted cross-sheet formula references use sheet IDs, not sheet names.
- Renaming a referenced sheet does not change persisted formula identity and does not break formula results.
- Formula edit/display text shows current sheet labels derived from IDs.
- Saving edited formula text converts user-visible sheet labels back into UUID-backed references.
- Deleting or missing a referenced sheet causes the relevant formula to show #REF!.
- Backend reload after rename preserves formula meaning through sheet IDs.
- Tests cover UUID-backed reference round trips, rename preservation, missing target sheet handling, and backend persistence reload.

title: Decide MVP persistence save behavior and storage scope
description:
Context:
This decision records the MVP persistence behavior chosen for downstream backend API, storage, frontend lifecycle, and integration beads. The implementation should not re-open these product choices while working those beads.

Decision:
- MVP persistence uses backend durability for raw workbook state. Frontend formula evaluation is authoritative; backend does not calculate formulas and does not persist evaluated formula results.
- App startup loads the current workbook from the backend before editing is allowed. If initial load fails, show a retry/error screen and block workbook editing until load succeeds. Do not assume the workbook is empty on load failure.
- A real empty database loads as an empty workspace with zero sheets.
- MVP uses autosave after committed state changes. No required Save button is part of MVP.
- Autosaved committed changes include sheet create, sheet rename, sheet position changes after frame movement, cell edit commits, row append, and column append. Transient in-progress edits are not saved until committed.
- After a successful initial load, autosave failures are non-blocking: keep current in-memory workbook state editable, keep frontend formula display optimistic, and show a visible app-level unsaved/save-failed status.
- MVP save indicator is app-level only, with states such as loading, saving, saved, and save failed/unsaved. Do not add per-cell save indicators in MVP.
- Persistence APIs should be per-entity update APIs plus a load API. They should cover cell content updates, sheet creation, sheet rename/metadata update, sheet position update, row append, and column append.
- Autosave queueing is per entity key. Each key has at most one running save and one queued latest replacement. If a new edit for the same key arrives while one is running, replace the queued value with the latest committed value. Different keys may save in parallel.
- Entity keys should be chosen to match persisted units: sheet id plus cell address for cell content, sheet id plus metadata/position field group for sheet changes, and sheet/workbook structural keys for row/column append and sheet creation.
- Backend storage shape is an engineering choice as long as the observable behavior and tests match this decision.
- Durable MVP fields are workbook/schema version, sheet ids, unique visible sheet names, sheet frame positions, row/column counts, and raw cell contents including raw formulas.
- Non-MVP persistence features are excluded: accounts, collaboration, import/export, multi-workbook management, viewport history, frame sizes, evaluated formula result storage, and durable offline queues across page reload.

Acceptance criteria:
- Downstream persistence beads reference this decision instead of making their own save/load semantics choices.
- Backend and frontend tests cover startup load success/failure, empty database, per-entity updates, autosave status, failed autosave after successful load, and durable MVP fields.

Test expectations:
No code test is required for this decision bead. Downstream implementation beads must add tests named in their own test expectations.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
plan/mvp.task.md, plan/features/persistence.md, backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace, frontend/src

Dependencies/blockers:

title: Migrate sheet IDs to backend-owned UUIDs
description:
Problem / goal:
Sheet identity is currently client-shaped enough that deletion and later reference-safe refactors can race or collide with optimistic frontend state. Move durable sheet identity to backend-owned UUIDs before adding deletion.

Implementation scope:
- Change POST /api/sheets so clients no longer send an id.
- Generate sheet UUIDs in the backend and expose them as UUID strings in API responses.
- Store sheet IDs in SQLite as 16-byte BLOB values with CHECK(length(id) = 16).
- Add the migration needed for the new storage shape. Existing persisted workbook data may be discarded during this migration.
- Update backend and frontend API types to use the backend UUID string as the saved sheet id.
- Make sheet creation temporarily non-optimistic until pending frontend IDs are introduced by the next bead.
- Keep formulas name-based in this bead; do not migrate formula references here.

Out of scope:
- Idempotent create requests.
- Pending local sheet IDs or queued pending-sheet mutations.
- Sheet deletion UI or delete API behavior beyond any compile-time contract changes required by UUID IDs.
- Formula reference storage by UUID.

Acceptance criteria:
- Creating a sheet succeeds without sending an id in the request body.
- The backend assigns a UUID and returns it as the sheet id.
- SQLite stores sheet ids as 16-byte BLOB values guarded by CHECK(length(id) = 16).
- Reloading a workbook preserves backend-generated sheet UUIDs.
- The frontend no longer invents durable saved sheet ids during creation.
- Existing formulas continue resolving by visible sheet name with current behavior.
- Migration behavior explicitly permits discarding existing sheet data.

Plan references:
- docs/plan/PROJECT_VISION.md
- docs/plan/mvp.task.md
- docs/plan/features/persistence.md
- docs/plan/features/sheet-frames.md

Testing expectations:

title: Keep focused cells fully visible below sticky headers during arrow navigation
description:
Problem / goal:
When navigating cells with arrow keys, the focused cell is scrolled into view, but moving up or left can leave it partially covered by the sheet column or row headers. The active/focused cell should be fully visible after keyboard navigation.

Implementation scope:
- Fix frontend sheet grid scroll/focus behavior for arrow-key cell navigation.
- Account for sticky or overlaying column and row headers when ensuring the focused cell is visible.
- Preserve existing scroll behavior where selected cells are already visible and avoid unnecessary jumps.

Out of scope:
- Redesigning grid header layout.
- Virtualization or large-grid performance work unless required for this bug.
- Multi-cell selection behavior unless it shares the same focus path.

Acceptance criteria:
- Arrow navigation to cells above the current viewport leaves the focused cell fully visible below the column header.
- Arrow navigation to cells left of the current viewport leaves the focused cell fully visible beside the row header.
- Existing right/down navigation and scroll-into-view behavior remains correct.
- The active cell indicator is not obscured by row/column headers after navigation.
- Add or update focused tests where practical; if layout limitations make jsdom insufficient, record manual verification expectations in the bead or implementation notes.

title: Implement cell editing commit and cancel behavior
description:
Context:
Users need to edit text, numeric-looking values, formulas, and clear existing content. Enter commits active edits, moving focus commits active edits, and Escape cancels active edits.

What to do:
Add cell edit mode, input handling, and commit/cancel semantics. Store committed raw cell content in workbook state and preserve previous content when editing is canceled. Treat committed empty input as clearing the cell by removing its stored cell entry from workbook state.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can enter edit mode for a selected cell.
- Text and numeric-looking values can be typed and committed as raw cell content.
- Formula-looking values beginning with `=` can be typed and committed as raw cell content without normalization.
- Pressing Enter commits the active edit.
- Moving focus to another cell or sheet commits the active edit.
- Pressing Escape cancels the active edit and restores prior content.
- Empty committed input clears the cell by removing the stored cell entry from workbook state; the cell renders blank afterward.
- Clearing an already-empty cell leaves workbook state unchanged apart from normal edit UI state.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests with user-event cover entering edit mode, committing with Enter, committing on focus movement, canceling with Escape, editing text, editing numeric-looking values, editing raw formula text, clearing existing text/numeric/formula content, and clearing an already-empty cell.

title: Fix Escape canceling cell edit breaking keyboard input
description:
Problem / goal:
When a user presses Escape while editing a cell, Sheetspace exits edit mode in a broken state: keyboard navigation stops responding, and subsequent entered text is not written into the cell. This regresses the core familiar spreadsheet editing flow.

Implementation scope:
- Fix the frontend grid/cell editing state transition for Escape during cell editing.
- Ensure canceling edit with Escape restores normal selection focus/keyboard navigation.
- Ensure subsequent typing after Escape starts or updates cell editing normally and commits entered text according to existing behavior.
- Keep the change scoped to this regression; do not redesign broader editing UX.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pressing Escape while editing a cell cancels the edit without leaving keyboard handling stuck.
- Arrow-key navigation works immediately after Escape from edit mode.
- Typing after Escape can enter/write cell content using the existing cell editing behavior.
- Existing commit/cancel behavior for Enter, blur, or other edit controls is not regressed.
- Add or update focused frontend tests for the regression where practical.

Plan references:
- docs/plan/PROJECT_VISION.md: familiar spreadsheet editing comes first.

Testing expectations:
- Run the relevant frontend test/compile target for the touched editing code, or record why not run.
close_reason: Completed: Escape cancel restores cell keyboard focus and regression coverage passes.

title: Resize sheet frames from borders and corners
description:
Context:
Sheet frames should be resizable directly in the workspace, using OS-window-style border and corner handles. The product plan calls out resizing sheets from edges or corners and persisting frame size and position.

Problem / goal:
Users need to adjust the visible size of each sheet frame in the 2D workspace without changing the sheet data itself. Resizing should feel like dragging the border of a normal desktop window.

Implementation scope:
- Add pointer interaction affordances for resizing sheet frames from left, right, top, and bottom borders.
- Add corner resize behavior that changes width and height together.
- Store updated frame width and height in workbook state so the values can be persisted by the normal workbook save path.
- Enforce practical minimum width and height constraints so the header and grid remain usable.
- Keep the opposite edge fixed when dragging a border or corner; when resizing from the left or top, update x/y as needed to match normal window resize behavior.
- Keep resize initiation scoped to the frame border/corner areas so grid editing and selection remain available in the sheet body.
- Make resize math work in workspace coordinates, including after pan and zoom.

Out of scope:
- Changing sheet cell data as part of resize.
- Editable frame titles, z-order controls, collapse/minimize/hide, grouping, or layout automation.
- Backend persistence schema changes unless the workbook persistence path already needs frame width/height at implementation time.

Acceptance criteria:
- A user can resize a sheet frame horizontally by dragging the left or right border.
- A user can resize a sheet frame vertically by dragging the top or bottom border.
- A user can resize both dimensions by dragging any corner.
- Resizing one frame does not affect other frames.
- Updated width and height are reflected in workbook state for persistence.
- Minimum size constraints prevent unusable or negative frame sizes.
- Sheet contents remain usable inside the resized frame.
- Resize gestures do not accidentally start grid editing, grid selection, or frame movement.
- Resize behavior remains correct after workspace pan and zoom.

Plan references:
- plan/features/sheet-frames.md
- plan/features/workspace.md

Testing expectations:
Frontend tests should cover edge resizing, corner resizing, independent frame sizes, minimum-size clamping, non-resize interactions in the sheet body, and pan/zoom coordinate behavior where feasible. Add a short manual verification note for pointer cursor and handle feel if those details are hard to assert in jsdom.

title: Add spreadsheet keyboard cell navigation
description:
Context:
The grid supports single-cell selection and basic editing, but there is no dedicated bead for spreadsheet-style keyboard navigation between cells. Keyboard navigation should make the grid usable without the mouse while preserving existing edit entry and commit/cancel behavior.

Problem / goal:
Users should be able to move the active cell with the keyboard, commit edits with spreadsheet-like movement semantics, and keep the active cell visible as navigation moves through the sheet.

Implementation scope:
- Add arrow-key navigation for the selected cell: left, right, up, and down.
- Clamp navigation at sheet bounds unless a separate structural-growth behavior is explicitly introduced by another bead.
- Keep the selected cell visible inside the sheet viewport by auto-scrolling as needed when keyboard navigation moves outside the visible area.
- In edit mode, make Tab commit the active edit and move selection one cell to the right.
- In edit mode, make Enter commit the active edit and move to the next row using spreadsheet-style tab-run behavior: return to the column where the current tab sequence started, then move down one row. If there is no active tab-run origin, move down in the same column.
- Preserve Escape cancel behavior.
- Preserve printable-key editing behavior for a focused selected cell.
- Keep behavior scoped to single-cell selection; do not introduce range selection.

Out of scope:
- Shift+Tab, Shift+Enter, Ctrl/Cmd+arrow, PageUp/PageDown, Home/End, copy/paste, fill handles, range selection, or structural row/column creation.
- Changing formulas, persistence, sheet frame movement, or sheet frame resizing.
- Cross-sheet keyboard navigation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pressing ArrowLeft/ArrowRight/ArrowUp/ArrowDown with a selected cell moves selection one cell in that direction when within sheet bounds.
- Arrow navigation at sheet bounds leaves selection on the nearest valid cell.
- Keyboard navigation commits any active edit before moving only where the intended interaction requires a commit; Escape still cancels without committing.
- Pressing Tab while editing commits the raw edit value and selects the cell to the right.
- Pressing Enter while editing commits the raw edit value and selects the next row at the tab-run origin column, or the same column if no tab-run origin exists.
- After Tab followed by Enter, selection lands on the next row at the first column of that tab sequence.
- The selected cell is scrolled into view when keyboard navigation moves beyond the visible sheet viewport.
- Navigation updates the active sheet/cell state and visible selection consistently.
- Keyboard navigation does not introduce range, row, or column selection.

Plan references:
- plan/features/grid-editing.md

Testing expectations:
Frontend tests should cover arrow-key movement in all directions, clamping at row/column bounds, Tab commit-and-right behavior, Enter commit-and-next-row behavior, Tab-run origin behavior, Escape cancel preservation, printable-key edit entry preservation, and scroll-into-view behavior where feasible.

title: Rewrite formula sheet references when sheets are renamed
description:
Context:
MVP sheet rename deliberately does not rewrite formulas. Formulas that reference an old sheet name show `#REF!` until edited. A later improvement should preserve formulas across rename by updating sheet-qualified references safely.

What to do:

Acceptance criteria:
- Renaming a sheet updates formulas that reference the old sheet name so they continue resolving to the renamed sheet.
- Rewrites handle quoted and unquoted sheet names.
- Rewrites do not alter formulas that reference other sheets or contain old-name text outside parsed references.
- The behavior is covered for same-sheet formulas, cross-sheet formulas, and formulas with parse errors.

Test expectations:

title: Reshape workbook API around sheet IDs and targeted sheet loads
description:
Problem / goal:
Mutation endpoints currently return the full workbook after most changes. This is simple but wasteful and blurs ownership between optimistic client state and server persistence. The API should return only the data needed to reconcile the mutation, and clients should fetch targeted sheet data when they need recovery from conflicts or stale state.

Implementation scope:
- Stop returning the full workbook from routine mutation endpoints.
- Remove ok: true from successful mutation responses.
- Keep errors as HTTP 4xx/409 responses with a simple { "error": "..." } body.
- Change GET /api/workbook so it returns workbook-level metadata and sheet IDs only, not full sheet payloads with cells.
- Add GET /api/sheets/{sheetId} to return one full sheet, including sheet metadata, revision, dimensions, frame data, z-index, and cells.
- Update startup/load flow so the client gets workbook sheet IDs, then fetches individual sheets through the sheet endpoint.
- Update conflict recovery so a 409 sheet-revision-conflict reloads only the affected sheet with GET /api/sheets/{sheetId}.
- If targeted sheet reload returns 404, the client should treat that sheet as deleted or unavailable.
- Preserve sheet-level optimistic locking with If-Match: <sheet.revision> for revisioned mutations.

Expected success response shapes:
- POST /api/sheets: return the created Sheet, because the client needs backend-owned ID, revision, defaults, and persisted metadata.
- PATCH /api/sheets/{sheetId}: return { "sheetId": "...", "revision": n }.
- PUT /api/sheets/{sheetId}/cells/{cellAddress}: return { "sheetId": "...", "revision": n }.
- POST /api/sheets/{sheetId}/rows: return { "sheetId": "...", "revision": n, "rowCount": n }.
- POST /api/sheets/{sheetId}/columns: return { "sheetId": "...", "revision": n, "columnCount": n }.
- DELETE /api/sheets/{sheetId}: return 204 No Content.

Out of scope:
- Do not change formula reference identity in this bead; that is tracked separately.
- Do not add realtime sync, subscriptions, multi-user presence, or server push.
- Do not introduce workbook-level optimistic locking unless a concrete need appears during implementation.
- Do not add pagination or viewport-based partial cell loading yet.

Acceptance criteria:
- Initial workbook API no longer returns every full sheet/cell payload; it returns enough workbook metadata and sheet IDs for the client to fetch sheets separately.
- New GET /api/sheets/{sheetId} returns a full single sheet and returns 404 for unknown sheets.
- Routine mutations no longer return the full workbook.
- Successful responses omit ok: true.
- Error responses use HTTP status plus { "error": "..." }.
- Frontend optimistic state updates continue to work using minimal mutation responses.
- Conflict handling reloads only the affected sheet.
- Existing sheet creation, rename, move, resize, z-order, cell edit, row append, column append, and delete flows still work.

Relevant files / areas:
- backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace/Main.kt
- backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace/WorkbookRepository.kt
- backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace/Workbook.kt
- frontend/src/workbookApi.ts
- frontend/src/useStartupWorkbookLoad.ts
- frontend/src/useWorkbookController.ts
- frontend/src/useEditQueue.ts

Testing expectations:
- Add backend API tests for new workbook summary response, targeted sheet response, minimal mutation responses, delete no-content response, and error body shape.
- Add frontend API client tests for all new response shapes.
- Add autosave/edit queue tests proving revision updates are reconciled from minimal mutation responses.
- Add conflict recovery test proving only GET /api/sheets/{sheetId} is called after conflict.
- Run make test and make compile.

title: Render sheet grid with row and column headers
description:
Context:
Each MVP sheet starts as an empty 10-column by 20-row grid and should display recognizable spreadsheet row and column headers.

Acceptance criteria:

- Row headers display 1-based row numbers.
- Column headers display Excel-style labels and handle labels beyond Z when dimensions grow.
- Empty cells are visible and addressable.
- Multiple sheet grids render independently inside their frames.

title: Store formula sheet references by UUID and display names to users
description:
Problem / goal:
Formula references currently resolve through visible sheet names, so renaming a sheet breaks stored formulas. Once sheets have stable backend UUIDs, persist sheet-qualified formula references by backend UUID while continuing to show and edit user-facing names.

Implementation scope:
- Store sheet-qualified formula references using target backend sheet UUIDs in the persisted/internal representation.
- Continue displaying and editing formulas with visible sheet names, including quoted names where required.
- Resolve edited formulas from names to backend UUIDs when possible, preserving current error behavior for unknown sheets.
- Ensure renaming a sheet no longer breaks formulas that already reference that sheet.
- Ensure missing or deleted target sheet references display the existing #REF! behavior.
- Cover quoted and unquoted sheet names, rename, missing targets, malformed formulas, and same-sheet formulas.
- Existing persisted workbook/formula data may be discarded for this migration; no migration of old name-based formulas is required.

Out of scope:
- Formula edits that reference unsaved `pending:<uuid>` sheets; that pending-save workflow belongs in the dependent pending-reference bead.
- Migrating existing name-based formulas or preserving existing persisted workbook data through this storage change.
- Broad formula grammar expansion beyond the reference forms already supported.
- Reference navigation UI, hover cards, colored dependency lines, or formula refactoring commands.
- Import/export formula compatibility.
- Backend idempotency.
- Persisting `pending:<uuid>` references as resolvable live sheet references.

Acceptance criteria:
- Persisted sheet-qualified references survive sheet rename because the stored target is the backend sheet UUID.
- Users still see and edit formulas using sheet names rather than UUIDs.
- Quoted and unquoted sheet names round-trip through edit/display behavior.
- Missing or deleted target sheets display the existing #REF! behavior without crashing evaluation.
- Malformed formulas still produce the appropriate parse/reference errors.
- Existing same-sheet formulas keep current behavior after the new storage shape is in use.
- Any storage migration for this bead may wipe existing persisted workbook/formula data rather than migrating old name-based references.

Plan references:
- docs/plan/PROJECT_VISION.md
- docs/plan/mvp.task.md
- docs/plan/features/formulas.md
- docs/plan/features/references-and-navigation.md
- docs/plan/features/persistence.md

Testing expectations:
- Tests cover quoted and unquoted sheet references, persistence round-trip in the new storage shape, sheet rename preserving references, missing or deleted target showing #REF!, malformed formulas, and same-sheet formulas unaffected.
- Include backend/frontend coverage according to where formula persistence and parsing live after implementation.
- Run make test and make compile, or document narrower checks if the full suite is not practical.

title: Handle optimistic-lock conflicts for sheet persistence updates
description:
Context:
Backend MVP persistence now stores one row per sheet and repository updates save whole sheet rows. Same-sheet concurrent edits can still conflict once frontend autosave and API calls run in parallel. title: Rename sheets with unique visible names
description:
Context:
MVP cross-sheet formulas resolve by visible sheet name, so users need a way to set and change sheet names while names remain unique. Formula text is not rewritten after rename in MVP; formula recomputation after rename is handled by the recompute bead.

What to do:
Add sheet rename interaction from the sheet frame/header or equivalent sheet identity UI. Validate proposed names before committing. On successful rename, update workbook state and visible frame identity while preserving all raw cell content verbatim.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can rename an existing sheet from sheet identity UI.
- Proposed sheet names are trimmed for validation and must be non-empty and unique within the workbook.
- Empty or duplicate rename attempts are rejected with a testable validation/error state and the old name remains active.
- Successful rename updates the visible sheet frame identity and workbook state.
- Successful rename preserves all raw cell content and does not rewrite formulas.
- Formulas using old sheet names are not modified by this bead; recomputation and resulting `#REF!` display are covered by the formula recompute/display beads.
- Rename state changes use the same workbook shape consumed by the frontend autosave bead, but autosave wiring is not implemented here.

Frontend tests cover successful rename, empty name rejection, duplicate name rejection, old name preservation after failed rename, visible frame identity update, workbook state update, and no formula text rewrite.

title: Delete sheets
description:
Problem / goal:
Users can create, arrange, rename, and edit sheets, but cannot remove them. Implement deletion after backend UUID identity and frontend pending IDs exist so deletion is safe for both saved and optimistic sheets.

Implementation scope:
- Add a delete action to the existing sheet context menu or equivalent sheet-frame controls.
- Delete saved sheets optimistically in frontend state, then persist through a backend delete endpoint/repository operation.
- Delete pending sheets locally when possible, or complete deletion after an in-flight create resolves.
- Drop obsolete queued work for deleted sheets.
- Preserve unrelated sheet state: cells, dimensions, positions, sizes, z-order, names, and active state except for minimal normalization required when the active sheet is deleted.
- Return a structured sheet-not-found backend error for attempts to delete an unknown saved sheet.
- Keep existing formula error behavior for references to deleted sheets; formula reference storage changes are handled by a separate bead.

Out of scope:
- Trash, undo, collaboration, account scoping, multi-workbook behavior, import/export behavior, and durable offline queue semantics.
- Reference-aware formula rewrites during deletion.
- Backend idempotency for repeated delete requests unless needed to preserve current API error contracts.

Acceptance criteria:
- A user can delete a saved sheet from the workspace.
- Deleted sheets disappear from the rendered workspace and sheet count immediately.
- Deletion persists through backend reload.
- Deleting one sheet preserves unrelated sheets and queued unrelated mutations.
- Pending sheets can be deleted before create and during in-flight create without stale mutations or resurrected UI state.
- Missing backend sheet delete attempts return a structured sheet-not-found error.
- Autosave/save status handles deletion consistently with other queued sheet mutations.

title: Add backend database storage for MVP workbook state
description:
Context:
The MVP requires created sheets, unique visible names, dimensions, raw cell content, formulas, and sheet positions to persist through a real backend database using a straightforward single-instance read/write path. Backend persistence stores raw workbook state only; frontend formula evaluation is authoritative.

What to do:
Add backend database setup and a persistence repository for the MVP single-workbook state. Include schema/version handling appropriate for the first persisted shape and keep the path simple for one local instance. The database technology and table/document shape may be chosen during implementation, but the externally observable behavior must match the persistence decision.

Acceptance criteria:
- Backend starts with an initialized real database, not in-memory frontend-only storage.
- Repository can load the current workbook, returning an empty workbook with zero sheets when the database is genuinely empty.
- Repository can save and reload sheets, stable sheet ids, unique visible sheet names, row/column counts, raw cell contents, raw formulas, and frame x/y positions.
- Repository supports per-entity updates needed by autosave: sheet create, sheet rename/metadata, sheet position, cell content, row append, and column append.
- Persisted data includes a version marker or clear migration hook for the first schema.
- Evaluated formula results are not persisted.
- No accounts, collaboration, import/export, multi-workbook management, durable offline queue, or non-MVP viewport history is introduced.

Test expectations:
Backend tests cover empty initial load, per-entity update round trips, durable fields, unique sheet-name persistence, formula raw text surviving persistence unchanged, evaluated result absence, and schema/version marker behavior.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
backend/build.gradle.kts, backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace, backend/src/test/kotlin/com/sheetspace, plan/features/persistence.md

title: Decide MVP formula semantics and explicit exclusions
description:
Context:
This decision records the MVP formula behavior chosen for downstream parser, evaluator, display, and persistence beads. The implementation should not re-open these product choices while working those beads.

Decision:
- Frontend formula evaluation is authoritative for MVP; the backend persists raw workbook state only.
- Formula text is stored verbatim after commit, including casing, spacing, quoting, and line breaks. Parser/evaluator code may normalize tokens in memory but must not write normalized formula text back to workbook state or persistence.
- Function names are case-insensitive for evaluation. `SUM`, `sum`, and `Sum` all work.
- Whitespace and newlines are allowed around formula separators: after `=`, around function names, parentheses, commas, colons, references, and cross-sheet separators. Whitespace inside an address token such as `A 1` remains invalid.
- `SUM` supports same-sheet and cross-sheet references. Cross-sheet syntax is Excel-like visible sheet-name syntax: `Inputs!A1`, `Inputs!A1:B3`, `'Sales Q1'!A1`, and `'Sales Q1'!A1:B3`.
- Sheet names used in formulas resolve against current visible sheet names. Missing sheets, malformed sheet references, out-of-bounds cells, and unresolved ranges produce cell-level errors.
- Sheet renames do not rewrite formula text in MVP; formulas using an old name show `#REF!` until edited.
- `SUM` is strict numeric: empty referenced cells contribute 0; plain decimal/integer cell text contributes its numeric value; non-empty non-numeric text in a referenced cell causes `#VALUE!`.
- Plain numeric formats only: trim leading/trailing whitespace and accept integers/decimals with optional leading minus sign, such as `12`, `-3`, `4.5`, and `0.25`. Reject comma separators, currency, percentages, dates, and scientific notation as text for formula purposes.
- Referenced formula errors propagate. If multiple referenced errors are encountered, return the first error in deterministic evaluation order documented by tests.
- MVP error labels are `#PARSE!`, `#NAME!`, `#REF!`, `#VALUE!`, and `#CYCLE!`. `#PARSE!` means invalid formula syntax; `#NAME!` means unsupported/unknown function; `#REF!` means unresolved sheet/cell/range reference; `#VALUE!` means a resolved value cannot be used numerically; `#CYCLE!` means a direct or indirect formula cycle.
- Detect direct and indirect cycles, including cycles across supported cross-sheet references. Cells participating in a detected cycle display `#CYCLE!`; unrelated formulas continue computing.
- Do not add arithmetic operators, string literals, percentages, named ranges, extra functions, reference visualization UI, or optimized dependency-graph recalculation for MVP unless another bead explicitly requires them.

Acceptance criteria:
- Downstream formula beads reference this decision instead of making their own semantics choices.
- Parser/evaluator/display tests cover verbatim formula storage, case-insensitive function names, whitespace/newline tolerance, same-sheet references, cross-sheet references, strict numeric SUM behavior, error propagation, and cycle detection.

Test expectations:
No code test is required for this decision bead. Downstream implementation beads must add tests named in their own test expectations.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
plan/mvp.task.md, plan/features/formulas.md, frontend/src

Dependencies/blockers:
None. Decision recorded; dependent implementation beads may proceed once their remaining dependencies are satisfied.

title: Restore spreadsheet-like cell editing and formula display
description:
Context:
A user reported that cells appeared non-editable in the browser and that visible SUM formulas were not calculating. The edit confusion came from the grid only entering edit mode on double-click or Enter after activation; normal spreadsheet typing did nothing. The visible SUM issue came from the frontend evaluator existing in the workbook model but not yet being used by the grid display.

What to do:
Make keyboard input on a focused/active grid cell enter edit mode in a familiar spreadsheet way while preserving existing double-click and Enter behavior. Printable keys should start editing with the typed character. Backspace/Delete should start an empty edit. Also wire the current frontend formula evaluator into grid display so formula cells show evaluated results outside edit mode while raw formula text remains available when editing.

Scope notes:
This overlaps with dependent formula display work tracked by sheetspace-8a3. The immediate user-reported browser bug was fixed here because the user explicitly requested continuing with both typing-to-edit and visible SUM calculation. Do not add dependency graph optimization, formula reference visualization, multi-cell selection, copy/paste, or broader keyboard navigation in this bug.

Acceptance criteria:
- Focusing/selecting a cell and typing a printable character opens the cell editor with that character.
- Pressing Enter on an active cell still opens the editor with existing raw content.
- Pressing Backspace/Delete on an active cell opens an empty editor and committing clears the cell.
- Existing double-click edit behavior continues to work.
- Formula cells display evaluator results outside edit mode.
- Editing a formula cell shows the raw formula text, not the calculated result.
- Committing referenced cell edits recomputes displayed formula results.
- Frontend tests cover the new keyboard entry and formula display paths.

Testing expectations:
Run frontend tests plus root checks when feasible.

title: Expose workbook load and per-entity persistence APIs
description:
Context:
The frontend needs a backend read/write path for durable MVP workbook state. Persistence uses a load API plus per-entity update APIs that match autosave keys. The backend persists raw workbook state only and does not calculate formulas.

What to do:
Add HTTP endpoints for loading the current workbook and updating MVP entities: cell content, sheet creation, sheet rename/metadata, sheet position, row append, and column append. Define request/response DTOs and validation for malformed payloads. Keep health endpoint behavior intact.

Acceptance criteria:
- Frontend can load the current persisted workbook from the backend before editing.
- Empty database state returns a valid empty workbook with zero sheets.
- Frontend can create a sheet with id/name/position through a per-entity API.
- Frontend can update raw cell content through a per-cell API.
- Frontend can rename a sheet and update sheet position through sheet-level APIs.
- Frontend can append rows and columns through structure update APIs.
- Invalid update payloads return an appropriate 4xx response without corrupting existing data.
- Backend responses provide enough success/failure information for app-level save status.
- Backend does not calculate or persist evaluated formula results.
- Health endpoint continues to work.

Test expectations:
Ktor application tests cover load-empty, create-sheet then load, cell update then load, rename then load, position update then load, row/column append then load, invalid payload handling, formula raw text persistence without evaluated results, and health endpoint regression.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace/Main.kt, backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace, backend/src/test/kotlin/com/sheetspace/ApplicationTest.kt

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by backend database storage.

title: Verify formula recompute after row and column append
description:
Context:
sheetspace-8a3 requires row/column append to update formula reference validity, but row/column append UI/state is still tracked separately in sheetspace-jfa. The existing formula display implementation recomputes from workbook state, but append-triggered behavior cannot be accepted until append behavior exists.

What to do:
When sheetspace-jfa implements append controls, add coverage that formulas referencing newly valid or newly invalid ranges recompute from the latest dimensions. Keep this focused on recompute/display behavior; do not add formula reference rewriting.

Acceptance criteria:
- Formula references that become valid after column append recompute against the new dimension.
- Formula references that become valid after row append recompute against the new dimension.
- Existing formula errors remain cell-level after append.
- Tests cover the append-triggered recompute path through the app or workbook state layer.

Relevant context:
sheetspace-8a3, sheetspace-jfa.

title: Display formula results while preserving raw formula edits
description:
Context:
Formula cells should show evaluated results outside edit mode but reveal and preserve the stored raw formula text while editing. Stored formula text is verbatim user input, including casing, spacing, quoting, and line breaks. Formula error cells must remain selectable and editable.

What to do:
Integrate formula display results with grid rendering and editing. When a formula cell is not being edited, render its frontend-evaluated value or cell-level error. When editing, show the stored raw formula string exactly as persisted.

Acceptance criteria:
- Formula cells render evaluated SUM results outside edit mode.
- Editing a formula cell shows the stored raw formula text verbatim.
- Committing a changed formula updates the display result through frontend recomputation.
- Mixed-case, spaced, and multiline formulas retain their raw edit text while still evaluating where syntactically valid.
- Formula errors render at the cell level without crashing the app.
- Cells displaying formula errors remain selectable and editable.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests cover raw formula preservation, mixed-case formula display/editing, whitespace/newline formula preservation, evaluated display, cross-sheet formula display, error display, selecting and editing an error cell, and recompute-visible behavior after changing referenced cells.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/grid-editing.md, plan/features/formulas.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by recompute logic and cell editing behavior.

title: Split workspace viewport and sheet frame App tests
description:
Parent context: sheetspace-865.

Problem / goal:
Workspace panning/zooming, frame rendering, frame drag/resize, and context-menu/pan interaction tests are currently embedded in App.test.tsx. Move them into a thematic App integration file while relying on hook/geometry tests for detailed math branches.

Implementation scope:
- Create App.workspaceFrames.test.tsx or equivalent.
- Move App-level tests for empty workspace rendering, positioned sheet frames, multiple frame rendering, toolbar pan/zoom controls, wheel zoom, workspace drag pan, representative frame drag, representative frame resize, frame resize autosave, context menu closure/interactions, and no implicit z-raise from drag/select/edit.
- Keep only representative integration tests for drag/resize math. Detailed scaled deltas, accumulated movement, min-size clamp, and top/left anchoring should be covered by useSheetFrameInteractions/workspaceGeometry tests where practical.
- Remove the moved tests from the monolithic App.test.tsx.

Important constraints:
- Do not change user-visible workspace/frame behavior.
- Preserve accessibility-oriented queries where they describe the user surface; use test ids only for non-semantic frame internals such as resize handles.
- Keep this bead scoped to workspace/frame tests; do not move cell editing or formula tests here.

Acceptance criteria:
- Workspace/frame App integration tests live in their own file.
- App.test.tsx is smaller and no longer owns this behavior area.
- Lower-level tests cover detailed geometry or interaction math that was removed from App-level coverage.
- The frontend test suite passes.

Suggested check:
- cd frontend && npm test
